chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D-1287 of 2026 (Ghulam Mustafa versus Province of Sindh & others) Date Order with signature of Judge Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi 1. For order on office objections 2. For hearing of main case Date of hearing and order: 20.4.2026 Mr. Abdul Aziz Kanhar advocate for the petitioner Mr. Asad Ahmed advocate for the respondent / KMC --------------------- ORDER Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. Petitioner Ghulam Mustafa has filed this Constitution Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 seeking following relief:- a. To declare that the petitioner is legally entitled for recovery of retirement benefits Gratuity etc. from the respondents and the respondents are bound to act under the framework provided in constitution of Pakistan and to obey the law being the statutory body incorporated under the provincial government. b. To direct the respondents to release retirements benefits Gratuity etc. forthwith without committing any further delay as the respondents being statutory body is bound to act in accordance with law and recovery of dues is the fundamental right of the petitioner and respondent is bound under principle policy to do so. Learned counsel for the Petitioner submits that Petitioner was appointed on 01.11.1982 as ex Destructor Cooly in BPS-02 in Municipal Public Health Department KMC, and was retired from service on 01.03.2021, and since then his pensionary benefits have not been released. Learned counsel, representing the KMC submits that the petitioner is regularly drawing monthly pension from the HBL Branch regularly, however, a cheque amounting to Rs.226,029/- dated 10.2.2026 has already been issued on account of pension fund and the pension commutation of Rs.700,000/- is required to be paid by the KMC, which will be paid within two weeks.

Learned counsel for the Petitioner submits that Petitioner was appointed on 01.11.1982 as ex Destructor Cooly in BPS-02 in Municipal Public Health Department KMC, and was retired from service on 01.03.2021, and since then his pensionary benefits have not been released. Learned counsel, representing the KMC submits that the petitioner is regularly drawing monthly pension from the HBL Branch regularly, however, a cheque amounting to Rs.226,029/- dated 10.2.2026 has already been issued on account of pension fund and the pension commutation of Rs.700,000/- is required to be paid by the KMC, which will be paid within two weeks. The request seems to be reasonable and acceded to. When confronted the above position of the case, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks disposal of this petition in terms of the statement so made by the counsel for the KMC. Petition stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Zahid/*
